paper_title: Lake Development Study using Sediment Analysis and Radiocarbon Dating

methods.method_name: Lake Sediment Analysis for Development Assessment
methods.description: This method uses the distribution of main deposit-forming layers, organic matter, and micro- and macroelements as indices of lake development phases. It primarily employs the lithostratigraphical method, which is based on determining the proportion of organic, chemogenic, and mineral matter in lake deposits. The method also involves classifying sediment types based on their composition according to unified international terminology.

steps.description: Identify main deposit-forming layers, organic matter, and micro- and macroelements in lake deposits to be used as indices of lake development phases.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify key components (layers, organic matter, elements) in lake sediment samples for use as development indices.
outputs: Identified indices (layers, organic matter content, element concentrations)

steps.description: Employ the lithostratigraphical method based on the proportion of ingredients of organic, chemogenic, and mineral matter in lake deposits.
steps.searchable_summary: Apply lithostratigraphical analysis based on sediment composition proportions.
outputs: Proportions of organic, chemogenic, and mineral matter

steps.description: Classify sediment composition using unified international terminology (sands, clays, lacustrine chalk, marl, sandy gyttja, clayey gyttja, detritus gyttja, calcareous gyttja, and peat) based on the determined proportions of components (organic matter, SiO2, Al2O3, CaCO3) and other characteristics.
steps.searchable_summary: Classify lake sediment types based on composition and component proportions.
inputs: Proportions of organic, chemogenic, mineral matter

steps.description: Use the distribution of main deposit-forming layers, organic matter, and micro- and macroelements, along with the lithostratigraphical classification, as indices of lake development phases.
steps.searchable_summary: Interpret sediment characteristics and lithostratigraphical data to infer lake development phases.

methods.description: This method involves obtaining radiocarbon datings of organic matter from lake deposits and converting these radiocarbon dates to calibrated ages using a specific calibration curve.

steps.description: Radiocarbon datings of organic matter were made in the Institute of Geological Sciences, National Academy of Sciences of Belarus (IGSB), in Kiev Radiocarbon Laboratory (Ki), in Vilnius Radiocarbon Laboratory (Vs), and Tartu Radiocarbon Laboratory (TA).
steps.searchable_summary: Obtain radiocarbon dates for organic matter from lake sediments.
inputs: Organic matter samples from lake deposits

steps.description: The radiocarbon dates were converted to calibrated ages (cal yr BP) at one sigma using the IntCal04 calibration curve (Reimer et al. 2004).
steps.searchable_summary: Convert radiocarbon dates to calibrated ages using the IntCal04 curve.
outputs: Calibrated ages (cal yr BP) at one sigma
